inputs.label: Water Source Type
inputs.businessContext: Source type affects cost and treatment requirements. Based on ISO 14046 water footprint principles.

inputs.label: Annual Water Consumption
inputs.businessContext: Total water used annually. Key input for water balance and cost calculations. Referenced from ISO 14046 and water audit standards.

inputs.label: Water Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost of water per cubic meter. Varies by source and region. Based on typical utility rates.

inputs.label: Treatment Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost of treating water to required quality. Includes filtration, chemicals, energy. Based on industry benchmarks.

inputs.label: Recycling Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of water recycled/reused. Higher rates reduce consumption and costs. Aligned with circular economy principles.

inputs.label: Water Stress Index
inputs.businessContext: Regional water scarcity indicator (0=low stress, 1=high stress). Based on WRI Aqueduct or similar. Influences risk and optimization priority.

inputs.label: Energy Intensity
inputs.businessContext: Energy required per cubic meter of water supplied/treated. Key for carbon footprint. Referenced from ISO 50001 energy management.

inputs.label: Energy Cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Cost of electricity. Used to calculate energy component of water cost.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly labor cost for water system operators. Based on industry wage data.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total labor hours dedicated to water management annually.

inputs.label: Maintenance Cost per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual maintenance cost for water infrastructure. Includes repairs, parts, etc.

inputs.label: Capital Investment
inputs.businessContext: Initial investment for water optimization projects (e.g., recycling system). Used for ROI calculations.

inputs.label: Project Lifetime
inputs.businessContext: Expected useful life of the investment. Used for depreciation and NPV.

inputs.label: Discount Rate
inputs.businessContext: Discount rate for NPV calculation. Reflects cost of capital. Based on corporate finance standards.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data. Affects output reliability. Low confidence may trigger conservative adjustments.

rules: if waterSource == 'desalinated' then energyIntensity >= 3 (desalination typically high energy)
if recyclingRate > 0 then treatmentCostPerUnit may be higher due to advanced treatment

thresholds.waterStressIndex > 0.7: High water stress region - optimization critical
thresholds.recyclingRate < 10: Low recycling rate - potential for improvement
thresholds.energyIntensity > 2: High energy intensity - consider energy efficiency measures

formulas.verdict: if npv > 0 then 'Invest' else 'Do Not Invest'
formulas.dataConfidenceAdjusted: if dataConfidence == 'low' then npv * 0.8 else if dataConfidence == 'medium' then npv * 0.95 else npv

hiddenLossDrivers: if waterStressIndex > 0.7 then 'High water stress region - optimization critical'
if recyclingRate < 10 then 'Low recycling rate - potential for improvement'
if energyIntensity > 2 then 'High energy intensity - consider energy efficiency measures'
if annualWaterConsumption > 100000 then 'Large consumer - detailed audit recommended'
suggestedActions: if recyclingRate < 20 then 'Invest in water recycling system'
if energyIntensity > 1.5 then 'Implement energy-efficient pumps and motors'
if waterStressIndex > 0.5 then 'Develop water conservation plan'
if npv < 0 then 'Reevaluate project assumptions or seek incentives'

premiumFeatures: CSV export of input data and results
